title: Pilostyles calliandrae
taxonomy: Pilostyles calliandrae is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Pilostyles and is a type of holoparasite, which means it obtains its nutrients by parasitizing another plant. Pilostyles calliandrae is classified as a member of the order Gentianales and the clade Asterids. The plant is native to Australia and is known to parasitize the roots of Calliandra plants. Its unique classification as a holoparasite allows it to thrive in environments where other plants may struggle to survive.
